title: Center pj search snippets on matched query
description: pj search --pretty can show session boilerplate such as AGENTS.md instructions instead of the query in context. Ensure content snippets are extracted around the actual query match and add regression coverage.

title: Fix port discovery parent project mis-association
description: A localhost:8000 process started for subreddit-analysis is being associated with the broader kevin project row, making the dashboard live link misleading.
close_reason: Port discovery now prefers the most specific containing project path, preventing parent project rows like /Users/kevin from claiming child project servers.

title: Fix live ports demo ergonomics
description: pj ports lacks a human-friendly pretty mode and the dashboard live-link demo is hard to verify when a server is started from the project directory.
close_reason: Added pj ports --pretty, macOS lsof cwd association for live ports, and uncached include_ports refreshes for the dashboard.

title: Fix pj search dropping newer matches after early content limit
description: pj search can miss relevant recent projects such as epic-odds for generic terms because fs_store.search_content stops scanning as soon as it has limit raw session matches, before sorting and collapsing sessions into projects. It should rank from the complete match set or otherwise avoid letting earlier roots crowd out newer matches.
close_reason: Implemented search sort modes, project scoping, multi-term matching, regex search, and fixed filesystem search limiting so newer matches are not crowded out by root order.

title: Implement runtime port discovery facade
description: Add a runtime_ports facade that detects local listening TCP ports and maps processes back to project paths when possible. Keep OS-specific commands and parsing contained to one module.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: pj.runtime_ports wraps lsof/ss/proc-style sources behind normalized records; tests characterize parser behavior with fixture output; failures return empty data with envelope error metadata instead of crashing; no mutations or process control are performed.
close_reason: Done: implemented pj.runtime_ports facade with lsof/ss/procfs parsers, normalized port records, graceful error envelopes, and pj ports CLI wrapper. Validation: python3 -m pytest tests/test_runtime_ports.py; python3 -m pytest; python3 -m pj ports --project definitely-not-a-project.

title: Define pj ports CLI contract
description: Define the CLI/API contract for local port discovery before implementation. Specify command names, JSON envelope shape, fields, confidence levels, platform support, and failure behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: contract covers pj ports, pj ports --project <query>, and census include-ports behavior; records include project_id/path, live_urls, pid, port, host, command, cwd when available, confidence, and source; readers must ignore unknown fields; no browser-only behavior is part of the contract.
close_reason: Done: documented pj ports, pj ports --project, and census include-ports contract with record fields, confidence levels, platform/failure behavior, and web API mapping. Validation: git diff --check -- README.md; python3 -m pytest tests/test_census.py.

title: Add live search to census web UI
description: Add a search endpoint and dashboard UI that expose pj search from the browser. Results should include project matches, session snippets, and resume affordances without changing pj search semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: GET /api/search?q=... calls pj.search.search; UI debounces input; results show project/session context and resume command; CLI pj search remains the source of truth; focused tests cover endpoint shape; Playwright validation exercises the served dashboard search flow and confirms result rows and resume affordances render.
close_reason: Done: added debounced live dashboard search backed by /api/search, separated census table filtering, rendered session snippets and resume commands, and kept pj search semantics in the shared search module. Validation: python3 -m pytest tests/test_census.py; python3 -m pytest; Playwright dashboard search flow.

title: Add project detail drawer
description: Clicking a census row should open a project detail drawer backed by pj show-equivalent shared logic. The drawer should show sessions, models, durations, notes, tags, priority, and resume command.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: project detail endpoint resolves by id/name/path using existing discovery logic; drawer renders recent sessions and metadata; no project state is stored in the browser except view state; tests cover resolver and response shape; Playwright validation clicks a census row and confirms the drawer opens, renders expected fields, and closes cleanly.
close_reason: Done: added a project detail drawer backed by shared pj show-equivalent resolution, with row click loading recent sessions, metadata, notes, tags, priority, and resume command. Validation: python3 -m pytest tests/test_census.py; python3 -m pytest; Playwright dashboard row-click drawer flow.

title: Define CLI-first web API contract
description: Document and test the web endpoint contract for pj census/search/show/chat/annotation surfaces. Keep endpoints as thin wrappers over shared modules and existing CLI semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: endpoints are mapped to existing CLI/shared functions; JSON response shapes are documented; writes go through append-only annotation APIs; no new runtime dependencies are introduced.
close_reason: Done: documented and tested CLI-first web API contract; added thin stdlib endpoints for census/search/show/chats/chat/annotations with annotation writes through pj.annotate and shared project session helper. Validation: python3 -m pytest (178 passed).

title: Map Yolobox /workspace sessions to host project identity
description:
## Objective

Teach project-juggler to use Yolobox workspace-mapping metadata to associate Codex sessions recorded as `/workspace` or `/workspace/<relative-subdir>` with the corresponding host checkout, while preserving the original recorded workspace path for diagnostics/provenance.

Do not start until `yolobox-pattern:YOLO-cc9` defines the exact mapping metadata format and where project-juggler can read it.

## Context

Current behavior groups projects by the recorded workspace string:

- `pj/parsers/codex.py` - Codex `session_meta.payload.cwd` and `turn_context.payload.cwd` parsing.
- `pj/fs_store.py` - `_group_by_workspace`, `list_projects`, `recent_session_counts`, `project_sessions`, `search_sessions`, `search_content`, `_session_to_dict`.
- `pj/cass_facade.py` - CASS `list_projects`, `recent_session_counts`, `project_sessions`, `search_sessions`, `search_content`; current SQL groups by `workspaces.path`.
- `pj/discover.py` - `project_id`, `resolve_project`, `resolve_project_for_cwd`.
- `pj/project_sessions.py` - `project_session_data` and resume command path selection.
- `pj/resume.py` - `full_resume_command`.
- `pj/search.py` - project-path scoping and session-hit merge behavior.
- `tests/test_pj.py`, `tests/test_census.py` - focused coverage.

Discovery commands:

- `rg -n "workspace|source_path|project_sessions|_group_by_workspace|full_resume_command" pj tests`
- `rg -n "workspaces w|source_path|source_id|origin_host|GROUP BY w.path|WHERE w.path" pj/cass_facade.py tests/test_pj.py`
- `rg -n "resolve_project|project_id|resolve_project_for_cwd" pj/discover.py tests`
- `rg -n "resume_cmd|project_session_data|print_status|print_search" pj tests`

Scratchpad/design references:

- `docs/plans/fs-store-design.md`
- `docs/cass-provider-watch.md`
- External blocker: `yolobox-pattern:YOLO-cc9`

## Required mapping contract

Use the mapping format produced by `yolobox-pattern:YOLO-cc9`.

The implementation must distinguish:

- Recorded workspace path: the literal path from the session, e.g. `/workspace`.
- Project identity path: the host checkout path used for grouping, IDs, annotations, and project resolution.
- Resume path: a host-routable path used by `pj resume`, `pj show`, `pj chats`, and search-result resume commands.

For `/workspace/<relative-subdir>`, resolve the identity/resume path by joining the mapped host checkout root with `<relative-subdir>` only when that subdirectory is itself the recorded session workspace. Do not collapse distinct host worktrees just because they share a basename.

## Scope

In:

- Filesystem-backed Codex sessions.
- CASS-backed sessions when the required mapping metadata is available.
- `pj list`, `pj search`, `pj show`, `pj chats`, `pj resume`, and project detail/search resume commands.
- Preservation of original recorded workspace spelling in diagnostic/provenance fields.
- Tests for mapped and unmapped sessions.

Out:

- Changing Yolobox behavior or mapping metadata format.
- Rewriting existing session files or CASS records.
- Merging unmapped `/workspace` sessions into host projects by basename or lexical guess.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Filesystem-backed Codex host sessions and mapped Yolobox `/workspace` sessions for the same host checkout appear as one project.
- [ ] Filesystem-backed `/workspace/<relative-subdir>` sessions map to the corresponding host subdirectory according to the Yolobox mapping contract.
- [ ] CASS-backed sessions use the same identity mapping when the required metadata is available.
- [ ] CASS-backed sessions fall back to current exact `workspaces.path` grouping when mapping metadata is missing.
- [ ] Original recorded workspace paths remain available in session diagnostics/provenance and are not globally overwritten.
- [ ] Resume commands use a host-routable path, never `cd /workspace`, for mapped sandbox sessions.
- [ ] Unmapped `/workspace` sessions remain visible as their own project and do not merge into unrelated projects.
- [ ] Distinct host worktrees with the same basename remain distinct.
- [ ] Existing lexical project resolution still works for ordinary host projects.

## Testing

Focused:

- `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py -k "codex or workspace or resume or cass"`

Regression:

- `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py tests/test_census.py`

Add or update tests covering:

- FS Codex host path plus mapped `/workspace` path collapse.
- FS Codex `/workspace/<relative-subdir>` mapping.
- Unmapped `/workspace` fallback remains separate.
- Distinct same-basename host worktrees remain separate.
- CASS mapped session collapse when metadata exists.
- CASS fallback to exact `workspaces.path` grouping when metadata is missing.
- `pj resume` / project detail resume command uses the host path for mapped sandbox sessions.

## Dependencies

Blocked by: `yolobox-pattern:YOLO-cc9`.

This issue is deferred so `bd ready` does not list it until the Yolobox mapping contract exists.

title: Route pj next through remote mode
description:
## Objective

Make `pj next` use the host service when `PJ_REMOTE_URL` is set while preserving local scheduling behavior and pretty rendering. This lets sandbox agents inspect the host-side work queue without owning the host project index.

## Context

- Source: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md#cli-behavior`
- Files:
  - `pj/cli.py` - `_cmd_next`
  - `pj/schedule.py` - local scoring heuristic
  - `pj/census_server.py` - `/api/next`
  - `pj/pretty.py` - `print_next`
  - `pj/envelope.py` - standard success/error envelope behavior
  - remote client helper/path added by `pj-55m.5` - after that ticket closes, update this issue with the exact module/function name for URL building, token headers, connection errors, and envelope errors
  - `tests/test_pj.py` and `tests/test_census.py` - next tests
- Discovery:
  - `rg "def _cmd_next|print_next|score_projects|/api/next" pj tests`
  - `rg "test_cli_next|test_census_server_next" tests`
  - `rg "next_p.add_argument|--limit" pj/cli.py`
  - `rg "PJ_REMOTE_URL|urlopen|Request|remote|_remote" pj tests`
  - After `pj-55m.5` closes, also search for the exact token env/header names it introduced.
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Scope

In:
- Remote mode for `pj next`.
- `limit` query mapping.
- Preserve local behavior when `PJ_REMOTE_URL` is absent.
- Preserve `--pretty` rendering from remote `data`.

Out:
- Changing schedule scoring.
- Adding browser next-queue behavior; it already exists.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Local `pj next` behavior is unchanged when `PJ_REMOTE_URL` is absent.
- [ ] With `PJ_REMOTE_URL`, `pj next --limit N` calls `/api/next?limit=N` using the remote client path from `pj-55m.5`.
- [ ] Remote success JSON prints the returned standard envelope `data`/`meta` without local scoring.
- [ ] Remote-mode tests assert `pj.cli.discover.discover` and `pj.cli.schedule.score_projects` are not called; scoring must happen only on the host `/api/next` service.
- [ ] Remote envelope errors and connection errors follow the standard CLI error behavior established by `pj-55m.5`.
- [ ] `pj next --pretty` renders remote `data` through `pretty.print_next`.
- [ ] Focused tests cover remote JSON, remote pretty, remote envelope error, and local no-remote regression.

## Testing

- Focused: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py -k "next and remote"`
- Regression: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py::test_cli_next_json tests/test_census.py::test_census_server_next_endpoint_uses_schedule_flow`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: `pj-55m.5`.
- Do not start this ticket until `pj-55m.5` is closed. After it lands, use the exact remote helper module/function and token/header names introduced there; do not infer names from this ticket.
- Blocks: host and sandbox setup docs.

## Notes

This is a read-only sensor. Do not add remote annotation actions here.

title: Document host and sandbox pj setup
description:
## Objective

Document the end-to-end workflow for running `pj` on the host and using the same CLI from a disposable sandbox through `PJ_REMOTE_URL`.

## Context

- Source: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`
- Files:
  - `README.md` - user-facing CLI, sandbox setup, and web API docs
  - `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md` - design/task graph scratchpad
  - `pj/cli.py` - actual flags and command names
  - Exact project-juggler skill path recorded by closed `pj-55m.4` - agent-facing workflow. If `pj-55m.4` does not record a concrete path, stop as blocked.
- Discovery:
  - `rg --files | rg '(^|/)SKILL\.md$|project-juggler|skills'`
  - `rg "census start|census serve|PJ_REMOTE_URL|host.docker.internal|pj health" README.md docs`
  - `rg "pj search|pj show|pj chats|pj chat|pj next" README.md docs`
  - `rg "PJ_READ_TOKEN|PJ_WRITE_TOKEN|PJ_REMOTE_TOKEN|Authorization" README.md docs pj`
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Scope

In:
- Host setup command.
- Sandbox env vars and example commands.
- Token/auth setup from the remote exposure/auth implementation.
- Clear note that annotation writes are actuators.
- `pj chats` example as the session-listing step before `pj chat`.
- README web API wording consistent with the final `pj-55m.7` ports outcome.
- Troubleshooting for missing host service or bad token.

Out:
- Implementing CLI/server behavior.
- Writing long conceptual docs already covered by the plan.
- Automatic Docker host detection.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] README includes host setup and sandbox setup.
- [ ] README examples include `pj health`, `pj search`, `pj show`, `pj chats`, `pj chat`, and `pj next`.
- [ ] README and skill both identify annotation writes as actuators.
- [ ] README and skill reflect the final auth token names and write policy.
- [ ] Host setup command matches the final `pj-55m.6` exposure/auth behavior; do not assume unauthenticated `--host 0.0.0.0`.
- [ ] README web API docs match the final `pj-55m.7` `/api/ports` decision.
- [ ] Skill and README do not contradict each other.

## Testing

- Focused docs check: `rg "PJ_REMOTE_URL|host.docker.internal|pj health|pj chats|PJ_REMOTE_TOKEN|PJ_READ_TOKEN|PJ_WRITE_TOKEN|Authorization" README.md docs <skill-path>`
- Skill/docs consistency check: `rg "PJ_REMOTE_URL|host.docker.internal|Actuators|Sensors" <skill-path> README.md`
- Command smoke: `python3 -m pj --help && python3 -m pj health`
- Playwright is not required for docs-only changes unless the edit changes rendered web UI, browser behavior, or the `pj census serve` HTTP surface.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: skill, auth enforcement, ports alignment, remote `search`, remote `show`, remote `chats`/`chat`, and remote `next` tasks.
- Blocks: None known.

## Notes

Do not document `--host 0.0.0.0` as normal until the exposure policy is enforced.

title: Route pj chats and pj chat through remote mode
description:
## Objective

Make `pj chats` and `pj chat` use the host service when `PJ_REMOTE_URL` is set, so a sandbox agent can list sessions for a host project and fetch bounded chat content without mounting the host session store.

## Context

- Source: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md#sandbox-workflow`
- Files:
  - `pj/cli.py` - parser flags around `chat`/`chats`, `_resolve_project_arg`, `_cmd_chats`, `_cmd_chat`, and `chat list` alias
  - `pj/census_server.py` - `/api/chats`, `/api/chat`, `/api/chat/<session_id>` routes and handlers
  - `pj/pretty.py` - session and chat renderers
  - remote client helper/path added by `pj-55m.5` - after that ticket closes, update this issue with the exact module/function name for URL building, token headers, connection errors, and envelope errors
  - `tests/test_pj.py` and `tests/test_census.py` - current chat tests
- Discovery:
  - `rg "def _cmd_chats|def _cmd_chat|chat list|--no-tools|--roles|--last" pj/cli.py tests/test_pj.py`
  - `rg "_handle_chats|_handle_chat|/api/chat" pj/census_server.py tests/test_census.py`
  - `rg "print_chat|print_sessions" pj/pretty.py`
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Scope

In:
- Remote mode for `pj chats`.
- Remote mode for `pj chat`.
- When `PJ_REMOTE_URL` is set, `pj chat list ...` follows the same remote path as `pj chats ...` because the alias rewrite happens before dispatch.
- In remote mode, `pj chats <project> ...` sends the provided project ref as the `project` query parameter to `/api/chats`.
- In remote mode, bare `pj chats` must not call `discover.resolve_project_for_cwd()`; it must return a standard CLI error requiring an explicit project or supported `--here`.
- In remote mode, `pj chats --here` is supported only if local `discover.resolve_project_for_cwd()` resolves a project; send that resolved project path as `project`. If local resolution fails, return the existing standard `Current directory is not inside a discovered project` error.
- Query mapping for `project`, `limit`, `session_id`, `roles`, `no_tools`, `all_branches`, `last`, and `offset`.
- Preserve local mode and `chat list` alias when `PJ_REMOTE_URL` is absent.

Out:
- Remote resume command execution.
- Remote annotation writes.
- Changing local chat parsing/session-store behavior.
- Automatic sandbox CWD to host project mapping beyond the explicit local `--here` resolution described above.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] With `PJ_REMOTE_URL`, `pj chats <project> --limit N` calls `/api/chats`.
- [ ] With `PJ_REMOTE_URL`, `pj chat <session_id> ...` calls `/api/chat/<id>`.
- [ ] With `PJ_REMOTE_URL`, ambiguous/missing project input for `pj chats` returns a standard CLI error rather than silently using sandbox-local discovery.
- [ ] Remote JSON success preserves the standard envelope shape returned by the remote helper; `--pretty` renders remote `data` through `pretty.print_sessions` or `pretty.print_chat`.
- [ ] Tests prove remote `pj chats --here` either sends the locally resolved project path or errors without silently falling back to sandbox-local session discovery.
- [ ] Tests prove remote `pj chat list ...` uses the same code path as remote `pj chats ...`.
- [ ] Local `pj chats`, `pj chat`, and `pj chat list` behavior is unchanged.
- [ ] `--pretty`, `--no-tools`, `--roles`, `--last`, `--offset`, and `--limit` behavior is covered by tests.
- [ ] Remote errors return standard CLI errors.

## Testing

- Focused: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py -k "remote and chat"` with new tests named like `test_cli_remote_chats_*` and `test_cli_remote_chat_*`.
- Regression: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py -k "cli_chats or cli_chat" tests/test_census.py -k "chat"`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: `pj-55m.5`.
- Before implementation, run `bd show pj-55m.5` and inspect the closing notes/code to identify the exact remote helper module/function. If no reusable remote helper exists, stop as blocked instead of adding an ad hoc HTTP stack.
- Blocks: host and sandbox setup docs.

## Notes

For sandbox agents, this is the key drill-down path after `pj search`.

title: Route pj show through remote mode
description:
## Objective

Make `pj show` use the host service when `PJ_REMOTE_URL` is set while preserving local-mode behavior, project-ref semantics, JSON envelope shape, session limit handling, and `--pretty` rendering.

## Context

- Source: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md#cli-behavior`
- Files:
  - `pj/cli.py` - `_cmd_show`
  - `pj/project_sessions.py` - local project detail helper
  - `pj/census_server.py` - `/api/show`
  - `pj/pretty.py` - project detail rendering
  - remote client helper/path added by `pj-55m.5` - URL building, token headers, connection errors, and envelope errors
  - after `pj-55m.5` closes, update this issue with the exact helper module/function name
  - `tests/test_pj.py` and `tests/test_census.py` - show/project detail tests
- Discovery:
  - `rg "def _cmd_show|resolve_project_detail|print_status|/api/show" pj tests`
  - `rg "test_cli_status|test_cli_show|test_census_server_show" tests`
  - `rg "project_session_data|resolve_project_detail" pj/project_sessions.py`
  - `rg "PJ_REMOTE_URL|PJ_REMOTE_TOKEN|urlopen|Request|remote|api/health" pj tests`
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Scope

In:
- Remote mode for `pj show` only.
- `project` and `sessions` query mapping.
- In remote mode, send the raw `args.project` value as the `project` query parameter and let the host `/api/show` endpoint perform project resolution; do not resolve against the sandbox/local project list.
- The hidden `pj status` alias may share this implementation path; preserve its existing local behavior and cover or explicitly document remote behavior.
- Preserve local behavior when `PJ_REMOTE_URL` is absent.
- Preserve `--pretty` rendering from remote `data`.

Out:
- Remote mode for `pj chats` or `pj chat`.
- Changing project fuzzy resolution semantics.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Local `pj show` behavior is unchanged.
- [ ] With `PJ_REMOTE_URL`, `pj show <project> --sessions N` builds a structured request to `/api/show` with URL-encoded `project=<project>` and `sessions=N`.
- [ ] Remote 400/404 or `success: false` responses print a standard envelope and exit `1`; connection failures use the remote client error behavior introduced by `pj-55m.5`.
- [ ] `--pretty` renders remote project details.
- [ ] Focused tests cover JSON and pretty remote paths.

## Testing

- After adding tests named like `test_cli_show_remote_*`: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py -k "show and remote"`
- Keep existing `test_cli_status_*` alias regression coverage.
- Regression: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_census.py -k "show"`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: `pj-55m.5`.
- Before implementation, run `bd show pj-55m.5` and inspect the closing notes/code to identify the exact remote helper module/function. If no reusable remote helper exists, stop as blocked instead of adding an ad hoc HTTP stack.
- Blocks: host and sandbox setup docs.

## Notes

Do not make `--here` for `show`; current CLI does not expose that flag.

title: Enforce remote server exposure policy
description:
## Objective

Implement the remote exposure/auth policy chosen in the remote exposure decision on the census server and process start/serve paths. Non-loopback serving must not silently expose annotation actuators.

## Context

- Source: `pj-55m.1` remote exposure decision and `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md#security-and-safety-rails`. This issue is BLOCKED until `pj-55m.1` is closed and records the exact token names, Authorization/header format, loopback versus non-loopback behavior, and annotation write policy. If `pj-55m.1` is not closed or any of those fields are missing, stop as blocked; do not choose policy here.
- Files:
  - `pj/census_server.py` - HTTP route handling, auth checks, control endpoint, annotation endpoints
  - `pj/census_process.py` - background start/status state
  - `pj/cli.py` - `pj census serve/start` flags and errors
  - `tests/test_census.py` - server endpoint tests
  - `tests/test_pj.py` - CLI/process behavior tests if CLI output changes
- Discovery:
  - `rg "PJ_CENSUS_CONTROL_TOKEN|PJ_READ_TOKEN|PJ_WRITE_TOKEN|Authorization|X-PJ" pj tests`
  - `rg "def do_GET|def do_POST|_handle_annotation|api/control/stop" pj/census_server.py`
  - `rg "def start|command = \\[|PJ_CENSUS_BACKGROUND" pj/census_process.py`
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Policy to Implement

Copy the closed `pj-55m.1` decision here before starting:
- Read token name:
- Write token name or remote-write disabled behavior:
- Client token/env name:
- Header format:
- Loopback behavior:
- Non-loopback bind behavior:
- Annotation write behavior:
- Control endpoint behavior:

## Scope

In:
- Server-side enforcement for read/write/control surfaces.
- Startup behavior for unsafe non-loopback serving as chosen in `pj-55m.1`.
- Structured envelope errors where requests reach the API layer.
- Focused tests for loopback default, non-loopback auth, bad token, good token, and write-gating behavior.

Out:
- Remote CLI read command routing.
- Remote annotation write CLI support.
- TLS, user accounts, or multi-tenant auth.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Non-loopback serving follows the `pj-55m.1` policy.
- [ ] Implementation exactly follows the closed `pj-55m.1` policy for read auth, write auth/disablement, header format, and unsafe non-loopback bind behavior.
- [ ] Read endpoints and annotation endpoints follow the separate read/write handling specified by `pj-55m.1`.
- [ ] Control endpoint remains protected by its control token.
- [ ] Unauthenticated and unauthorized requests fail predictably.
- [ ] Tests cover both safe defaults and configured remote exposure.

## Testing

- Focused: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_census.py -k "health or annotation or control or auth"`
- Broader: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_census.py tests/test_pj.py`
- UX/browser: because this changes the census HTTP surface used by the dashboard, run Playwright validation per README web UX rules, or explicitly document why the final code path does not affect rendered UI/browser interactions.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: `pj-55m.1`.
- Blocks: host and sandbox setup docs.

## Notes

Prefer a small helper for auth checks in `pj/census_server.py`; avoid scattering token parsing across every handler.

title: Align /api/ports contract with server
description:
## Objective

Apply the `pj-55m.3` decision exactly so README and census server behavior agree about port data. Before editing, run `bd show pj-55m.3`; if it is not closed or does not specify the chosen direction and exact contract/doc wording, stop without changes and report that this ticket remains blocked.

## Context

- Source: `pj-55m.3` decision once closed, plus `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md` Task G
- Files:
  - `README.md` - web API contract table
  - `pj/census_server.py` - server route table and handlers
  - `pj/runtime_ports.py` - runtime port sensor
  - `pj/cli.py` - `pj ports` command
  - `tests/test_runtime_ports.py` and `tests/test_census.py` - test coverage
- Discovery:
  - `rg "api/ports|include_ports|pj ports|runtime_ports" README.md pj tests`
  - `rg "def _cmd_ports|runtime_ports.ports|ports_p" pj/cli.py pj/runtime_ports.py`
  - `rg "ThreadingHTTPServer|_api_request|include_ports" tests/test_census.py`
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Scope

In:
- The chosen code/doc change from `pj-55m.3`.
- Tests for the chosen endpoint or documentation contract.

Out:
- Changing runtime port detection heuristics.
- Remote CLI support for `pj ports` unless explicitly chosen by `pj-55m.3`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Preflight confirms `bd show pj-55m.3` is closed and contains the exact implement-or-defer decision.
- [ ] README and server behavior agree.
- [ ] The implementation/doc change matches the explicit closed `pj-55m.3` decision; this task makes no new contract choice.
- [ ] If implemented, `/api/ports` returns the standard envelope and wraps existing `pj ports` semantics.
- [ ] If deferred, README states port data is available via `/api/census?include_ports=1`.
- [ ] Focused tests cover the chosen behavior.

## Testing

- If implemented: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_census.py tests/test_runtime_ports.py`
- If docs-only defer: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_runtime_ports.py` and `rg "api/ports|include_ports" README.md docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: `pj-55m.3`.
- Blocks: host and sandbox setup docs.

## Notes

Do not let this task grow into a broader port-detection refactor.

title: Route pj search through remote mode
description:
## Objective

Make `pj search` use the host service when `PJ_REMOTE_URL` is set while preserving local behavior, JSON envelope shape, query semantics, and `--pretty` rendering.

## Context

- Source: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md#cli-behavior`
- Files:
  - `pj/cli.py` - `_cmd_search`
  - `pj/search.py` - local search semantics
  - `pj/census_server.py` - `/api/search` query contract
  - `pj/pretty.py` - `print_search`
  - remote client helper/path added by `pj-55m.5` - URL building, token headers, connection errors, and envelope errors
  - `tests/test_pj.py` and `tests/test_census.py` - current search tests
- Discovery:
  - `rg "def _cmd_search|SEARCH_HELP|looks_like_regex|print_search" pj tests`
  - `rg "_handle_search|/api/search|query=query" pj/census_server.py tests/test_census.py`
  - `rg "test_cli_search|test_census_server_search" tests`
  - `rg "PJ_REMOTE_URL|PJ_REMOTE_TOKEN|urlopen|Request|remote" pj tests`
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Scope

In:
- Remote mode for `pj search` only.
- URL/query encoding for `q`, `limit`, `sort`, `project`, `match`, and `regex`.
- Preserve existing `--here` behavior in remote mode: resolve the current project before the request, reject `--project` with `--here`, error outside a discovered project, and send the resolved path as the `project` query parameter.
- Preserve local mode when `PJ_REMOTE_URL` is absent.
- Preserve `--pretty` output by rendering remote `data`.

Out:
- Remote mode for other commands.
- New search ranking or field selection.
- Automatic Docker host discovery.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Local `pj search` tests still pass unchanged.
- [ ] With `PJ_REMOTE_URL`, `pj search` calls `/api/search`.
- [ ] Multi-term queries become repeated `q` parameters.
- [ ] Remote `--here` sends `project=<resolved path>` and JSON mode preserves `meta.here` and `meta.project`.
- [ ] Remote server envelopes with `success: false`, connection failures, HTTP failures, and invalid JSON failures print a standard `success: false` envelope with `meta.error` and exit nonzero.
- [ ] Implementation reuses the remote HTTP helper/client introduced by `pj-55m.5`; do not create a second ad hoc remote stack.
- [ ] `--pretty` renders remote results.

## Testing

- Focused: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py -k "search and remote"`
- Name new focused tests with both `search` and `remote` so they are selected by `-k "search and remote"`; e.g. `test_cli_search_remote_*`.
- Add focused CLI tests for remote URL construction, repeated `q`, remote error envelope handling, remote `--pretty`, and remote `--here`.
- Regression: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py::test_cli_search_json tests/test_census.py::test_census_server_search_endpoint_maps_cli_query_semantics`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: `pj-55m.5`.
- Do not start until `pj-55m.5` is closed. Before implementation, run `bd show pj-55m.5` and inspect the closing notes/code to identify the exact remote helper module/function. If no reusable remote helper exists, stop as blocked instead of adding an ad hoc HTTP stack.
- Blocks: host and sandbox setup docs.

## Notes

Keep regex hint behavior consistent. If the server provides `meta.hint`, the CLI should preserve it in JSON mode and render equivalent human guidance in pretty mode.

title: Add project-juggler agent skill
description:
## Objective

Add the project-juggler agent skill at the location chosen by the skill-location decision. The skill must teach agents how to use `pj` as a sensor, how to detect/use host-backed remote reads from a sandbox, and which commands are actuators requiring explicit user intent.

## Context

- Source: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md#agent-skill-contract`
- Files:
  - final skill path recorded by the closed `pj-55m.2` decision; do not start this task while `pj-55m.2` is open
  - `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md` - source contract and sandbox workflow
  - `README.md` - current CLI and web API behavior
  - `pj/cli.py` - current command names and flags
- Discovery:
  - `rg "pj search|pj show|pj chats|pj chat|pj next" README.md pj/cli.py`
  - `rg "PJ_REMOTE_URL|PJ_REMOTE_TOKEN|PJ_READ_TOKEN|PJ_WRITE_TOKEN" docs README.md pj`
  - `rg "note|tag|prioritize|archive" README.md pj/cli.py pj/annotate.py`
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Scope

In:
- Skill frontmatter and concise body.
- Sensor list: `search`, `show`, `chats`, `chat`, and `next`. Do not include `ports` unless this ticket is updated to depend on the ports contract decision.
- Actuator list: `note`, `tag`, `prioritize`, `archive`.
- Sandbox workflow using explicit `PJ_REMOTE_URL`.

Out:
- CLI implementation changes.
- Remote server auth implementation.
- Long tutorial docs duplicating README.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Skill identifies read sensors and annotation actuators.
- [ ] Skill tells agents to prefer JSON envelopes and reserve `--pretty` for human-facing output.
- [ ] Skill explains that local sandbox `pj` may not see host chat history.
- [ ] Skill shows `PJ_REMOTE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:8765`.
- [ ] Skill reflects the exact token names and header behavior recorded by the closed `pj-55m.1` decision; do not invent or use draft token names from this ticket if the decision is still open.

## Testing

- Documentation-only focused validation: `rg "PJ_REMOTE_URL|host.docker.internal|Actuators|Sensors" <skill-path>`
- Broader validation: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py::test_cli_search_help_teaches_query_strategy`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: `pj-55m.1` and `pj-55m.2`.
- Blocks: host and sandbox setup docs.

## Notes

Do not include agent framework prompts, orchestration protocols, or behavior that would mutate annotations unless the user explicitly asks for that mutation.

title: Add pj health and health-only remote client path
description:
## Objective

Add `pj health` as the first vertical remote-read slice. Local mode should report local capability and local census service status; remote mode should call `GET /api/health` using `PJ_REMOTE_URL` and print the remote server envelope unchanged.

## Context

- Source: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md#cli-behavior`
- Files:
  - `pj/cli.py` - argparse setup and command dispatch
  - `pj/census_process.py` - local background server status helper
  - `pj/census_server.py` - `GET /api/health`
  - `pj/envelope.py` - standard envelope helpers
  - `tests/test_pj.py` - CLI tests
  - `tests/test_census.py` - existing `pj census status` regression test
- Discovery:
  - `rg "census status|_cmd_census|census_process.status|api/health" pj tests`
  - `rg "def build_parser|elif args.command|envelope.ok|envelope.err" pj/cli.py pj/envelope.py`
  - `rg "PJ_REMOTE_URL|urlopen|Request|urllib" pj tests`
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Scope

In:
- `pj health` parser command.
- Local status envelope.
- Remote `GET /api/health` call when `PJ_REMOTE_URL` is set.
- Token header support using names chosen in `pj-55m.1`.
- Focused tests with mocked HTTP/status calls.

Out:
- Routing other read commands through remote mode.
- Starting/stopping the census server.
- Auto-detecting Docker host URLs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Local `pj health` returns `envelope.ok({"mode": "local", "census": census_process.status(), "remote_url": None})`.
- [ ] `PJ_REMOTE_URL=... pj health` calls `/api/health`.
- [ ] Remote `pj health` validates the response is JSON and prints the remote server envelope unchanged, rather than wrapping it in another envelope.
- [ ] Remote invalid JSON, URL errors, and non-2xx HTTP errors return `envelope.err(..., source="remote")` with `meta.error`.
- [ ] After `pj-55m.1` is closed, use its chosen client env var/header names exactly; do not invent auth names in this ticket.
- [ ] Existing `pj census status` behavior is unchanged.

## Testing

- Focused: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py tests/test_census.py -k "health or census_status"`
- Broader: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_pj.py tests/test_census.py`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: `pj-55m.1`. Do not start until it is closed and has recorded the client env var name and HTTP header format for remote read auth.
- Blocks: command-specific remote read slices.

## Notes

Keep this slice small. A tiny remote HTTP helper may be introduced here, but it only needs to support health until later slices extend it.

title: Decide remote exposure and auth policy
description:
## Objective

Choose and record the security policy for using the census server as a host-side service reachable from disposable Docker/yolobox sandboxes. The decision must cover reads, annotation writes, control endpoints, token names, and behavior for non-loopback binds.

## Context

- Source: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md#security-and-safety-rails`
- Files:
  - `pj/census_server.py` - current HTTP routes and unauthenticated annotation POST handling
  - `pj/census_process.py` - background server lifecycle and control token handling
  - `README.md` - documented web API contract and web UX maintenance rules
  - `tests/test_census.py` - existing endpoint tests
- Discovery:
  - `rg "PJ_CENSUS_CONTROL_TOKEN|annotations|do_POST|do_GET" pj/census_server.py pj/census_process.py`
  - `rg "GET /api|POST /api|web API contract|annotations" README.md`
  - `rg "annotation|control|health|search endpoint" tests/test_census.py`
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Scope

In:
- Decide whether all non-loopback serving requires auth.
- Decide whether reads and writes use separate tokens.
- Decide whether annotation writes are disabled remotely by default or gated.
- Decide how unsafe `pj census serve/start --host 0.0.0.0` behaves.

Out:
- Implementing the policy in code.
- Designing remote annotation writes beyond the minimum safe exposure rule.

## Rationale

Record a short rationale for the chosen policy. It must explain why the policy is safe for browser users, sandbox agents, and non-loopback host exposure while preserving `PJ_CENSUS_CONTROL_TOKEN` for stop/control behavior.

## Alternatives Considered

Record at least these alternatives and why they were accepted or rejected:
- Loopback remains unauthenticated; non-loopback requires read auth.
- Separate read and write tokens.
- Read-only remote mode with annotation writes disabled outside loopback.
- Single shared token for all non-control endpoints.
- Refusing non-loopback binds unless explicit auth/config is present.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The decision is written into this plan or a linked ADR/decision section.
- [ ] Token names and header format are specified.
- [ ] Loopback versus non-loopback behavior is specified.
- [ ] Annotation write behavior is specified separately from read behavior.
- [ ] Rationale and alternatives considered are recorded.
- [ ] Downstream implementation tasks no longer need to choose a security model.

## Testing

- Documentation-only task; no test command required.
- Optional sanity check: `rg "PJ_READ_TOKEN|PJ_WRITE_TOKEN|Authorization|non-loopback" docs README.md`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: None - can start immediately.
- Blocks: skill, health, and server exposure implementation tasks.

## Notes

The current control endpoint already uses `PJ_CENSUS_CONTROL_TOKEN`; do not weaken that path. Treat browser, sandbox-agent, and user input as untrusted at the HTTP boundary.

title: Decide project-juggler skill location and distribution
description:
## Objective

Choose where the project-juggler agent skill should live and how it should be installed or discovered. The decision must let an implementation agent add the skill without inventing project-specific packaging conventions.

## Context

- Source: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md#agent-skill-contract`
- Files:
  - `README.md` - user-facing install and command documentation
  - `AGENTS.md` - project-local agent instructions
  - `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md` - draft skill contract
  - `docs/plans/pj-project-juggler.md` - earlier SKILL.md summary and agent-test framing
- Discovery:
  - `rg -n "SKILL.md|skill|AGENTS.md|project-juggler" README.md AGENTS.md docs engineering_core.md`
  - `rg --files | rg '(^|/)(SKILL.md|skills/|AGENTS.md)$'`
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Scope

In:
- Choose one skill location and distribution path.
- State whether the skill is project-local docs only, a packaged Codex skill, or both.
- State any required frontmatter/name constraints.

Out:
- Writing the skill content.
- Implementing installer or marketplace behavior.

## Rationale

Record a short rationale for the chosen skill location and distribution path. The rationale must explain how a zero-context agent will discover the skill and how the choice avoids project-specific packaging ambiguity.

## Alternatives Considered

Record at least these alternatives and why they were accepted or rejected:
- Project-local docs only.
- A packaged Codex skill with `SKILL.md` frontmatter.
- Both project-local reference and packaged skill distribution.
- Referencing the skill from `AGENTS.md` versus relying only on skill discovery.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The chosen skill path is written down.
- [ ] The chosen distribution/install expectation is written down.
- [ ] The decision says whether `AGENTS.md` should reference the skill.
- [ ] The decision records these fields explicitly: chosen skill path, distribution/install expectation, whether `AGENTS.md` should reference it, required frontmatter, and brief rationale.
- [ ] The decision is recorded in `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md` under Task B or a linked decision section, and the skill-location open question is resolved or points to that decision.
- [ ] Rationale and alternatives considered are recorded.
- [ ] Downstream skill implementation does not need to choose a location.

## Testing

- Documentation-only task; no test command required.
- Optional sanity check: `rg "project-juggler|SKILL.md|skills/" docs README.md AGENTS.md`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: None - can start immediately.
- Blocks: project-juggler agent skill implementation.

## Notes

If choosing a packaged Codex skill, specify the exact skill path, required `SKILL.md` frontmatter fields (`name: project-juggler`, `description: ...`), and whether install/discovery is project-local, packaged, or both.

title: Decide /api/ports HTTP contract
description:
## Objective

Choose whether `GET /api/ports` should be implemented on the census server or removed/deferred from the documented web API contract. This resolves the current README/server mismatch before implementation.

## Context

- Source: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md` Task D, especially `### Task D: Decide /api/ports HTTP Contract`; related mismatch note under `## Ambiguity Flattening`.
- Files:
  - `README.md` - web API contract table documents both `GET /api/census?refresh=1&include_ports=1` and `GET /api/ports?project=...`
  - `pj/census_server.py` - `Handler.do_GET` route dispatch currently has no `/api/ports` branch
  - `pj/runtime_ports.py` - `ports()` returns the existing standard envelope to reuse if implementing
  - `pj/cli.py` - defines `pj ports` parser args and `_cmd_ports`
  - `tests/test_runtime_ports.py` and `tests/test_census.py` - existing coverage
- Discovery:
  - `rg "api/ports|include_ports|pj ports|runtime_ports" README.md pj tests`
  - `rg "def _cmd_ports|ports_p|runtime_ports.ports" pj/cli.py pj/runtime_ports.py`
  - `rg "parsed.path == \"/api/ports\"|include_ports" pj/census_server.py`
- Scratchpad/plan: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Scope

In:
- Decide implement-now versus document-defer.
- Record the decision under Task D in `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`, or create/link a clearly named decision section from Task D.
- State the reason for the choice in one or two sentences, enough for `pj-55m.7` to implement without re-deciding.
- If implementing, define query parameters and response envelope by mapping to `pj ports`.
- If deferring, define replacement documentation text pointing users to `GET /api/census?include_ports=1`.

Out:
- Implementing the chosen behavior.
- Changing port detection semantics.

## Rationale

Record why the chosen direction is appropriate for the first remote-read contract. Prefer implement-now only if `/api/ports` should expose the same envelope/semantics as `pj ports`; prefer document-defer if port data should remain available only as census enrichment through `GET /api/census?include_ports=1` for now.

## Alternatives Considered

Record at least these alternatives and why they were accepted or rejected:
- Implement `GET /api/ports` now using existing `runtime_ports.ports()` semantics.
- Defer/remove the separate endpoint and document `GET /api/census?include_ports=1` as the supported path.
- Keep the README row as-is without server support.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Task D in `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md` contains the selected direction and rationale, or links to a decision note.
- [ ] If implementing, the note specifies exact endpoint path, query params, success envelope metadata, and error behavior by mapping to `pj ports`.
- [ ] If deferring, the note specifies exact README replacement wording and says to remove or mark deferred the `GET /api/ports` row.
- [ ] Rationale and alternatives considered are recorded.
- [ ] `pj-55m.7` can apply the decision without choosing implement-versus-defer.

## Testing

- Documentation-only task; no test command required.
- Optional sanity check: `rg "api/ports|include_ports" README.md docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: None - can start immediately.
- Blocks: /api/ports contract alignment implementation.

## Notes

This is independent of host-backed chat search. It is included because a remote CLI contract should not advertise an endpoint the server does not route.

title: Host-backed pj access for sandbox agents
description:
## Objective

Track the work needed to make `pj` usable both on the host and inside disposable Docker/yolobox sandboxes, with sandbox agents reading the broader host-side project/chat index through a host-backed `pj` service.

## Context

- Source: `docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md`
- Primary surfaces: `pj` CLI, `pj census` HTTP server, project-juggler agent skill, README setup docs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Remote exposure/auth policy is decided and implemented.
- [ ] Project-juggler agent skill exists in the chosen location.
- [ ] `PJ_REMOTE_URL` read mode supports `health`, `search`, `show`, `chats`, `chat`, and `next`.
- [ ] README documents safe host and sandbox setup.
- [ ] Dependency graph can be consumed by `bd ready` without hidden planning work.

title: Convert sandbox pj plan into task-template breakdown
description: Apply the task breakdown process to docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md by replacing the rough proposed tickets with atomic task-template-ready slices, exact onboarding, tests, and dependency graph.
close_reason: Converted docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md from rough proposed tickets into process-compliant task breakdown with HITL decisions, AFK vertical slices, onboarding context, tests, and dependency graph.

title: Add ticket breakdown to sandbox pj access plan
description: Add the proposed independently-grabbable ticket breakdown for host-backed sandbox pj access to the existing markdown plan.
close_reason: Added the suggested independently-grabbable ticket breakdown to docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md.

title: Bump project-juggler version to 0.3.0
description: Prepare the dashboard and live port feature work for demo/release by updating package version metadata.
close_reason: Version metadata bumped to 0.3.0 and tests updated.

title: Document host-backed pj access for sandbox agents
description: Capture the proposed SKILL.md-first design for making pj usable both on the host and inside disposable yolobox sandboxes, with host-backed read access to the broader chat index.
close_reason: Documented the SKILL.md-first host-backed pj design for sandbox agents in docs/plans/pj-sandbox-agent-access.md and linked it from README.

title: Bump pj version after chats command
description: Update the visible pj CLI/package version after adding the cwd chat listing command and verify the version command exposes the new version.
close_reason: Bumped package and CLI version to 0.2.2 and verified with tests.

title: Add cwd chat listing command
description: Need a direct way to list chats/sessions for the project containing the current working directory, matching the user's goal of seeing all chats here without resolving the project manually.
close_reason: Implemented pj chats and pj chat list alias with cwd project resolution, docs, and tests.

title: Bump pj version after regex discoverability change
description: Update the visible pj CLI/package version after the regex discoverability improvement and verify the version command exposes it.
close_reason: Bumped pj version to 0.2.1 and verified CLI version command

title: Improve pj search regex discoverability
description: Make --regex easier to discover from initial search attempts, especially regex-looking queries run without the flag.
close_reason: Implemented regex discoverability hints and tests

title: Audit pj search timestamp source
description: Investigate why pj search reports an older relative timestamp than recent chat interaction time. Trace the timestamp used for search results and identify whether it reflects session creation, last file modification, indexed metadata, or another source.
close_reason: Fixed pj search/session timestamps to use session last activity (ended_at) when available; added regression tests and verified the original search now reports recent activity.

title: Add CLI lifecycle controls for census web server
description: Provide human- and agent-friendly commands to start the census web dashboard as a background process, inspect its status, and stop it safely from the CLI.
acceptance_criteria: census CLI supports background start/status/stop; status and start/stop emit structured JSON by default; process metadata is stored predictably; tests cover lifecycle behavior
close_reason: Implemented census background start/status/stop CLI lifecycle with structured JSON output, local health/control endpoint, process metadata, docs, and tests.

title: Add cwd-scoped pj search shortcut
description: Add pj search --here to infer the current project from the command working directory and constrain search to that project path. Use longest path containment among discovered projects and fail clearly if cwd is not inside a known project.
close_reason: Added pj search --here to infer the current project from cwd using longest discovered project path containment; documented and tested success/error behavior.

title: Make search help teach agent-friendly query strategy
description: Make pj search --help and no-result output discover regex, project scoping, multi-term search, sort modes, and the difference between quoted phrase searches and separate terms so agents choose better searches.
close_reason: Made pj search help teach project scoping, multi-term search, regex, sort modes, and phrase-search pitfalls; added no-result hint for quoted multi-word searches.

title: Add pj --version and bump release version
description: Expose the package version through the CLI with pj --version and bump the package from 0.1.0 to the next obvious pre-1.0 feature release version.
close_reason: Added pj --version and bumped the pre-1.0 feature release version to 0.2.0.

title: Codex resume should not require --resume
description: In pj --pretty human-readable output, resume commands are currently printed like: cd /Users/kevin/Notes/Areas/health && codex --resume 019e16a8-558b-74b0-8bfd-de9052b1867. The desired output is: cd /Users/kevin/Notes/Areas/health && codex resume 019e16a8-558b-74b0-8bfd-de9052b1867. Update the command formatting path so pj emits the positional 'codex resume <session-id>' subcommand form instead of the deprecated/incorrect '--resume' flag form, and remove any human-readable help/output references that tell users to run codex --resume.
close_reason: Changed Codex resume command formatting from codex --resume <id> to codex resume <id>, updated CLI/help docs, and covered with tests.

title: Reconcile remote-only packaging metadata after main branch restore
description: Local main is the current canonical project tree, while origin/master has unrelated older history with a few possibly useful files. Review and selectively port useful remote-only pieces such as pyproject.toml metadata/classifiers and .gitignore differences; decide whether scripts/separate-project-juggler-host.sh and .claude/settings.json should stay archived or be dropped; then make main the GitHub default branch if appropriate.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: compare current main against origin/master for pyproject.toml, .gitignore, .claude/settings.json, and scripts/separate-project-juggler-host.sh; port only still-useful metadata or ignore entries without reverting the current version/package layout; explicitly decide whether the Claude settings and separation script are retained, archived, or dropped; verify package import/tests still pass; confirm origin/HEAD/default-branch cleanup status is recorded or completed.

title: Render live localhost links in census dashboard
description: Show live localhost links in the census web UI for projects with strong runtime port matches.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: pj census serve can request port enrichment; dashboard shows compact clickable localhost links; links open in a new tab; no client-side port scanning or discovery exists; unavailable port data leaves the UI clean; Playwright validation confirms live links render from server-provided live_urls and are absent when no port data is present.
close_reason: Done: dashboard requests census port enrichment and renders compact server-provided live_urls as new-tab localhost links while empty rows stay clean and link clicks do not open the detail drawer. Validation: python3 -m pytest tests/test_census.py; python3 -m pytest; Playwright live dashboard link rendering/new-tab/no-drawer flow.

title: Document port discovery boundaries
description: Document why live port links are implemented as an optional runtime sensor surfaced by pj rather than as core project discovery.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: docs explain sensor vs actuator boundary, CLI-first flow, platform caveats, confidence levels, privacy/security posture, and when this should become a separate tool instead of pj core.
close_reason: Done: documented port discovery as an optional local runtime sensor with sensor/actuator boundaries, CLI-first flow, platform caveats, confidence semantics, privacy/security posture, and split-out criteria. Validation: git diff --check -- README.md.

title: Add static web app detection hints
description: Add lightweight static detection for projects that look like web apps, separate from live runtime port detection.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: detector identifies common markers such as package.json scripts, Vite/Next/Astro/Svelte configs, Flask/FastAPI-style Python projects, and static HTML apps; output is a hint with evidence, not a hard classification; tests cover representative fixture directories.
close_reason: Done: added static web app hints with evidence for package scripts/dependencies, framework configs, Flask/FastAPI markers, and static HTML; surfaced hints through discovery and census rows while keeping live port detection separate. Validation: python3 -m pytest tests/test_web_hints.py tests/test_pj.py::test_discover_with_cass tests/test_pj.py::test_discover_refreshes_cached_web_hints_from_project_files tests/test_census.py::test_normalize_project_maps_detail_fields; python3 -m pytest.

title: Overlay live ports on census data
description: Add optional census enrichment that overlays runtime port matches onto project rows as live_urls and port metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: pj census supports an include-ports option or equivalent shared-function parameter; row shape includes live_urls and live_port_count when requested; default census remains fast and unchanged unless ports are requested; tests cover strong match, no match, and weak/ambiguous match behavior.
close_reason: Done: added optional census port enrichment via pj census --include-ports and /api/census?include_ports=1, with rows carrying ports, live_urls, and live_port_count while default census avoids runtime port discovery. Validation: python3 -m pytest tests/test_census.py; python3 -m pytest.

title: Add live localhost port links to web UX
description: Surface local web servers that appear to belong to pj projects. This should be implemented CLI-first as a runtime sensor and then rendered as live links in the census dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Epic is complete when the child issues deliver a CLI-visible ports sensor, optional static web-app hints, census overlay data, dashboard rendering, and documentation. Browser code must only render server-provided live_urls; pj must remain usable without the web server or port discovery.

title: Add session transcript viewer
description: From project detail sessions, allow opening a readable transcript backed by pj chat semantics with role filters and a hide-tools option.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: transcript endpoint calls get_store().get_session with the same options as pj chat; UI supports role filtering, last-N, and hide-tools; large sessions do not block initial census rendering; tests cover filtering parameters; Playwright validation opens a transcript from project detail and exercises role, last-N, and hide-tools controls.
close_reason: Done: added lazy project-detail transcript viewer backed by /api/chat with role, last-N, and hide-tools controls. Validation: tests/test_census.py, full pytest suite, and Playwright transcript flow.

title: Add annotation actions to web UI
description: Add lightweight project triage actions in the dashboard: note, prioritize, tag, and archive. All writes must go through existing append-only annotate functions.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: POST endpoints call pj.annotate functions; UI updates rows after successful writes; annotations remain append-only in pj data; dangerous/archive actions require explicit confirmation; tests cover each mutation endpoint using an isolated PJ_DATA_DIR; Playwright validation exercises note/tag/priority controls and the archive confirmation flow against isolated test data.
close_reason: Done: added project detail annotation actions for note, tag, priority, and confirmed archive using append-only POST endpoints with row/detail refresh. Validation: tests/test_census.py, full pytest suite, diff check, and Playwright annotation flow with isolated PJ_DATA_DIR.

title: Document web UX maintenance rules
description: Document how to extend the web UX without violating pj's CLI-first architecture or engineering_core.md.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: README or docs explain CLI-first layering, endpoint-to-CLI mapping, no-parallel-state rule, contribution checklist, and when Playwright browser validation is required for web UI changes; documentation references pj census serve and future web features.
close_reason: Done: documented web UX maintenance rules for CLI-first layering, endpoint mapping, no parallel UI state, contribution checklist, and Playwright validation criteria. Validation: README diff review and git diff --check.

title: Add next queue tab
description: Add a dashboard tab backed by pj next so the web UX can show the ranked work queue alongside the census.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: GET /api/next calls existing schedule/discover flow; UI shows score, reason, state, priority, and project link; CLI pj next remains behaviorally unchanged; tests cover endpoint metadata; Playwright validation switches to the next queue view and confirms ranked rows render without breaking the census view.
close_reason: Done: added /api/next backed by discover/schedule plus a dashboard Next Queue tab with ranked rows and project links. Validation: tests/test_census.py, CLI next/schedule focused tests, full pytest suite, diff check, and Playwright next queue/census switch flow.

title: Web UX roadmap
description: Build the pj web experience as an optional, CLI-first cockpit for project juggling. The web UX must consume shared pj modules and existing CLI contracts rather than creating a parallel product surface.
acceptance_criteria: Epic is complete when the dashboard supports search, project detail, next queue, transcript viewing, and annotation actions while preserving full CLI functionality without a server.

title: Add live census dashboard
description: Implement pj census serve as a stdlib local dashboard backed by live pj project data. It should serve the census UI without embedded stale data, expose a JSON endpoint, cache detailed scans, and refresh only when session signatures change.
close_reason: Implemented pj census JSON and live stdlib dashboard server with signature-aware refresh; tests pass.
